When the user asks you to create a pull request, follow these steps carefully:

1. First, run a git status command to see all untracked files. IMPORTANT: Never use the -uall flag as it can cause memory issues on large repos.
2. Then, run a git diff command to see both staged and unstaged changes that will be committed.
3. Check if the current branch tracks a remote branch and is up to date with the remote, so you know if you need to push.
4. Run a git log command and git diff base...HEAD to understand the full commit history.
5. Analyze all changes and draft a pull request title and summary.
6. Create PR using gh pr create.
PR workflow: 1) git status (no -uall) 2) git diff staged+unstaged 3) check remote tracking 4) git log + diff base...HEAD for full history 5) draft title (<70ch) + summary 6) gh pr create
